Document:
# Antagonists Antagonists are all those, who work against the laws and the established order. The Antag system in the Final Frontier however, works significantly different from the antagonist system of vanilla SS14. Antagonists are not a specific role, but rather can be played by anyone in any way they want. However, a set of special rules apply.
It is also important to know, that Antagonists are not necessarily evil, but they are usually at odds with other characters, the law or common goals. Antagonists can be simple thiefs, traitors, pirates or scammers, up to players promoting rebellion. They can be drug dealers, petty criminals or even engange in activities such has extorsion, human trafficking, abudction and taking hostages or homicide.
Antags cause conflict and trouble, while they also provide for some fear and mystery in Space Station 14. They enrich roleplay and make sure, players never get to lenient when it comes to security. They also give those who work in law enforment or other legal jobs, something to do. Generally, Antags, big or small, pose a challenge and a threatto the Reache's [color=#cb0000]Security[/color] forces, private and public alike.
Players can expect Antags to act in violation of Space Law and minding their own businesses. They work to achieve their own goals, using whatever means they have at their disposal, including theft, fraud, subterfuge, deception, violence and murder.
These bad actors are sometimes dangerous gangs or belong to powerful [color=#ff0000]Pirate Cartels[/color], but they can also be part of other criminal groups, or petty criminals, working on their own.
## Various Antagonists Antagonists can take many forms, like: - [textlink="Traitors" link="Traitors"] who work against the interests of their crew, either for someone else or for their own gain. - [textlink="Thieves" link="Thieves"] steel items, gear, or anything else of value to enrich themselves or to fund their own activities. - [textlink="Rebels" link="Rebels"] are those, who are against local law and order and try to overthrow it. - [textlink="Pirates" link="Pirates"] operate on their own as part of a gang or cartel and engage in all kind of criminal activity. - [textlink="Spies" link="Spies"] are masters of espionage and sabotage with special gear, whose goal is to obtain secret information or equipment. - [textlink="Scammers" link="Scammers"] that cheat and defraud others for their own gain, using deception and manipulation. - [textlink="Petty Criminals" link="Petty Criminals"] that engage in minor criminal activities, but still can be an issue.
The above list is not exhaustive, but it gives an idea of the different types of Antagonists that can be found in the Final Frontier. Generally players can play whatever they want, as long as they follow the server rules and the special [textlink="Guide to Criminal Gameplay" "Criminal Guide"].
